{
  "title": "Landlord Withholding Your Security Deposit? Here's How to Get It Back Fast",
  "meta_description": "Your landlord withholding your security deposit? Learn your tenant rights and get your deposit back in 60 seconds with Deposits-Back.com.",
  "content_outline": [
    "The Silent Heist: Why Landlords Get Away With Withholding Deposits",
    "Know Your Rights: State Timelines and What Landlords Can Actually Deduct",
    "From Frustration to Action: Writing a Demand Letter That Works",
    "The 60-Second Fix: How Deposits-Back.com Puts Legal Pressure on Your Landlord"
  ],
  "key_points": [
    "Most states require landlords to return security deposits within 14–30 days and provide an itemized list of any deductions",
    "Normal wear and tear—such as minor carpet fading or small nail holes—cannot legally be deducted from your deposit",
    "A professionally written demand letter is the fastest, cheapest way to force compliance before escalating to small claims court"
  ],
  "cta": "Stop waiting and hoping. For just $19.99, generate a professional security deposit demand letter in 60 seconds—no sign-up, no subscription, no stress. Visit Deposits-Back.com now and make your landlord pay attention.",
  "target_audience": "Frustrated US renters whose landlords are withholding, delaying, or unlawfully deducting from their security deposit and need immediate, affordable legal leverage without hiring a lawyer",
  "word_count_target": 800,
  "social_hooks": [
    {
      "hook": "POV: It's day 45 after move-out and your landlord is still 'processing' your $2,000 deposit...",
      "format": "TikTok/Reel",
      "visual_direction": "Split screen: left side shows tenant checking empty mailbox daily; right side shows landlord ghosting texts. Transition to tenant smirking at phone.",
      "script": "You moved out. You cleaned everything. Now your landlord is 'inspecting again' for the third time. Stop begging. Send a legal demand letter from Deposits-Back.com. It takes 60 seconds, costs 19 bucks, and suddenly landlords remember how to write checks. Link in bio.",
      "sound_suggestion": "Trending sad-to-empowered audio transition"
    },
    {
      "hook": "Can your landlord legally keep your deposit for 'cleaning'? Let's check the actual law.",
      "format": "TikTok/Reel",
      "visual_direction": "Green screen of state statute text. Landlord text bubble saying '$800 cleaning fee.'",
      "script": "Landlord says 'normal wear and tear' is coming out of your deposit? That's illegal in most states. But they bank on you not knowing that. This $19.99 letter from Deposits-Back.com cites the law for you and demands every dollar back. No lawyer. No subscription. Just results.",
      "sound_suggestion": "Oh no oh no oh no no no / record scratch transition"
    },
    {
      "hook": "If your landlord won't return your deposit, do NOT threaten to sue them yet. Do this instead.",
      "format": "TikTok/Reel",
      "visual_direction": "Fast-paced text-on-screen with trending captions. Pointing to phone screen showing Deposits-Back.com.",
      "script": "Step one: stop sending angry texts. Step two: go to Deposits-Back.com. Step three: answer a few questions and get a professional demand letter in under a minute. Step four: send it certified mail. Step five: watch your landlord panic and pay up. All for $19.99. No sign-up needed.",
      "sound_suggestion": "Trending step-by-step or checklist audio"
    },
    {
      "hook": "My landlord tried to charge me $1,200 for fake damages. Here's the letter that got my full deposit back.",
      "format": "TikTok/Reel",
      "visual_direction": "Storytime format with photo evidence of 'damages' vs move-in checklist. Screen recording of letter generation.",
      "script": "Storytime. My landlord said I broke a fridge that was already broken. They withheld my entire deposit. Instead of crying, I spent $19.99 on Deposits-Back.com. The letter cited my move-in photos and state law. I got my full deposit back in eight days. Save this before you need it.",
      "sound_suggestion": "Trending storytime beat"
    },
    {
      "hook": "Landlords have 14–30 days to return your deposit. If they miss this deadline, you could win double or triple.",
      "format": "TikTok/Reel",
      "visual_direction": "Countdown
